attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify each load-bearing claim by re-reading the cited files myself. Let me start by reading the key files.

content.content:
205	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS="rbi_hm"
206	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES="rbi_rq"
207	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES="rbi_es"
208	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS="rbi_df"
209	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_LODES="rbi_ld"
210	
211	  # GAR Lode Layout (fetched-side universal capture — see RBSL)
212	  # One Lode = one GAR package named rbi_ld/<kind-letter><stamp>; that package
213	  # IS the atomic-delete unit (single `packages delete`). Members and provenance
214	  # ride as TAGS within the one package, never as /-path-segments (GAR has no
215	  # subtree delete). Stamp matches the hallmark second-granular form YYMMDDHHMMSS.
216	  #
217	  # Kind letters (reserved set; only bole is implemented this pace):
218	  #   b  bole | t  tool | r  reliquary | w  wsl | vw  podvm-wsl | vn  podvm-native
219	  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE="b"
220	
221	  # Member/provenance tags. The rbi_ sprue marks strings from RB's domain:
222	  # RB's authored lexicon (bole, vouch) and RB-measured-from-content values
223	  # (the digest). It does NOT mark foreign-cued strings — the sanitized-origin
224	  # tag is UNSPRUED (origin is a vessel cue), computed at capture, not a constant.
225	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE="rbi_bole"            # uniform greppable handle (bole singleton)
226	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH="rbi_vouch"          # one-per-Lode provenance envelope
227	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX="rbi_sha256-"  # canonical OCI digest tag: rbi_sha256-<full-hex>
228	
229	  # Provenance envelope (:rbi_vouch) — two honest trust grades, declared per Lode.
230	  # bole captures the durable-upstream grade; podvm-* will carry the recorded grade.
231	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED="verified-against-published"
232	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED="recorded-at-acquisition"
233	  readonly RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA="rbld-vouch-1"    # near-term unsigned, schema-versioned
234

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle GAR Layout - Implementation
20	#
21	# Kindle constants for GAR categorical-namespace path construction.
22	# Callers interpolate the three root constants with hallmark/date/anchor and
23	# basename literals at the call site — no subshell, no function call overhead.
24	#
25	# Shape:
26	#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/<hallmark>/<basename>:<hallmark>"
27	#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/<date>/<tool>:<date>"
28	#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/<anchor>:<anchor>"
29	#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_DEPOT_FACTS_ROOT}/<filename>:<tag>"
30	#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/<kind-letter><stamp>:<member-tag>"
31	
32	set -euo pipefail
33	
34	# Multiple inclusion detection
35	test -z "${ZRBGL_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgl multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
36	ZRBGL_SOURCED=1
37	
38	######################################################################
39	# Internal Functions (zrbgl_*)
40	
41	zrbgl_kindle() {
42	  test -z "${ZRBGL_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgl already kindled"
43	
44	  # Category constants come from rbgc.
45	  zrbgc_sentinel
46	
47	  # Root segments — category only. Callers append '/<id>/<basename>:<tag>'.
48	  readonly RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS}"
49	  readonly RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES}"
50	  readonly RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES}"
51	  readonly RBGL_DEPOT_FACTS_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS}"
52	  readonly RBGL_LODES_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_LODES}"
53	
54	  readonly ZRBGL_KINDLED=1
55	}
56	
57	zrbgl_sentinel() {
58	  test "${ZRBGL_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgl not kindled - call zrbgl_kindle first"
59	}
60	
61	# eof
62

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - lifecycle REST (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
20	#   divine — enumerate Lodes / inspect one Lode's members (read-only)
21	#   banish — delete a whole Lode (Director credentials)
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	
25	######################################################################
26	# External Functions (rbld_*)
27	
28	rbld_divine() {
29	  zrbld_sentinel
30	
31	  local -r z_touchmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
32	
33	  buc_doc_brief "Divine Lodes — enumerate all Lodes, or inspect one Lode's members (read-only)"
34	  buc_doc_oparm "touchmark" "Lode stamp to inspect (e.g., b260602120000); omit to enumerate all Lodes"
35	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
36	
37	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
38	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
39	    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
40	  local z_token=""
41	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
42	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
43	
44	  if test -z "${z_touchmark}"; then
45	    buc_step "Enumerating Lodes under ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/"
46	    zrbfc_list_anchors_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}"
47	
48	    if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
49	      buc_info "No Lodes found under ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/"
50	      buc_success "Divine complete — 0 Lodes"
51	      return 0
52	    fi
53	
54	    # Kind-letter legend, printed once so rows carry no repeated per-row column.
55	    # A touchmark's leading letter is its kind (b260602075327 -> bole); the
56	    # reader decodes the prefix from this key. One entry per implemented kind.
57	    local -r z_kind_fmt="    %-3s %-10s %s\n"
58	    echo ""
59	    printf "  Kinds (touchmark prefix):\n"
60	    printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}" "bole" "upstream OCI image, consumed as a FROM line"
61	
62	    # Load the touchmark list fully before iterating: the per-Lode tags fetch
63	    # spawns curl (via rbuh), and a child touching stdin would consume the
64	    # loop's remaining input. Load-then-iterate keeps that FD closed.
65	    local z_touchmarks=()
66	    local z_touch=""
67	    while IFS= read -r z_touch || test -n "${z_touch}"; do
68	      test -n "${z_touch}" || continue
69	      z_touchmarks+=("${z_touch}")
70	    done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
71	
72	    local -r z_row_fmt="  %-15s %s\n"
73	    echo ""
74	    printf "${z_row_fmt}" "TOUCHMARK" "IMAGE"
75	    printf "${z_row_fmt}" "---------------" "--------------------------------------"
76	
77	    local z_idx=0
78	    local z_pkg=""
79	    local z_pkg_encoded=""
80	    local z_tags_url=""
81	    local z_enum_infix=""
82	    local z_resp_file=""
83	    local z_image_file=""
84	    local z_image=""
85	    for z_idx in "${!z_touchmarks[@]}"; do
86	      z_touch="${z_touchmarks[$z_idx]}"
87	
88	      # One tags-list per Lode. IMAGE is the unsprued fingerprint tag
89	      # <sanitized-origin>-<sha10>; it is located via the sha10 taken from the
90	      # rbi_sha256-<hex> member tag, so Director semantic names (also unsprued)
91	      # cannot masquerade as the fingerprint. Per-Lode infix preserves each
92	      # response for forensics.
93	      z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touch}"
94	      z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
95	      z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
96	      z_enum_infix="rbld_divine_enum_${z_idx}"
97	      rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_enum_infix}"
98	      rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touch}" "${z_enum_infix}"
99	      z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_enum_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
100	
101	      z_image_file="${ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX}enum_${z_idx}_image.txt"
102	      jq -r --arg dp "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" '
103	        [.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")] as $names
104	        | ([$names[] | select(startswith($dp)) | ltrimstr($dp)[0:10]][0]) as $sha10
105	        | ([$names[] | select((startswith("rbi_") | not) and ($sha10 != null) and endswith("-" + $sha10))][0]) // "(no fingerprint)"
106	      ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_image_file}" \
107	        || buc_die "Failed to extract fingerprint for Lode ${z_touch}"
108	      z_image=$(<"${z_image_file}")
109	      test -n "${z_image}" || buc_die "Empty fingerprint extraction for Lode ${z_touch}"
110	
111	      printf "${z_row_fmt}" "${z_touch}" "${z_image}"
112	    done
113	    echo ""
114	    buc_info "Total Lodes: ${#z_touchmarks[@]}"
115	    buc_success "Divine complete"
116	    return 0
117	  fi
118	
119	  # Inspect depth: list the member tags on one Lode package.
120	  buc_step "Inspecting Lode ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
121	  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
122	  local -r z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
123	  local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
124	  local -r z_tags_infix="rbld_divine_tags"
125	
126	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_tags_infix}"
127	  rbuh_require_ok "List tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}" "${z_tags_infix}"
128	
129	  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_tags_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
130	  local -r z_tags_file="${ZRBLD_DIVINE_PREFIX}tags.txt"
131	  jq -r '.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_tags_file}" \
132	    || buc_die "Failed to extract member tags for Lode ${z_touchmark}"
133	
134	  if ! test -s "${z_tags_file}"; then
135	    buc_die "No member tags found under ${z_pkg} — Lode not present in registry"
136	  fi
137	
138	  echo ""
139	  printf "  %s\n" "MEMBER-TAG"
140	  printf "  %s\n" "------------------------------"
141	  local z_count=0
142	  local z_tag=""
143	  while IFS= read -r z_tag || test -n "${z_tag}"; do
144	    test -n "${z_tag}" || continue
145	    printf "  %s\n" "${z_tag}"
146	    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
147	  done < "${z_tags_file}"
148	  echo ""
149	  buc_info "Total members: ${z_count}"
150	  buc_success "Divine complete — Lode ${z_touchmark}"
151	}
152	
153	rbld_banish() {
154	  zrbld_sentinel
155	
156	  local -r z_touchmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
157	
158	  buc_doc_brief "Banish a Lode — delete the whole rbi_ld/<touchmark> GAR package"
159	  buc_doc_param "touchmark" "Lode stamp to delete (e.g., b260602120000)"
160	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
161	
162	  test -n "${z_touchmark}" || buc_die "Touchmark parameter required"
163	
164	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
165	  local z_token=""
166	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
167	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
168	
169	  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_touchmark}"
170	  local -r z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
171	
172	  # Verify presence before delete so banish reports a clean not-found.
173	  buc_step "Verifying Lode present: ${z_pkg}"
174	  local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1"
175	  local -r z_probe_infix="rbld_banish_probe"
176	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_probe_infix}"
177	  rbuh_require_ok "Probe Lode ${z_touchmark}" "${z_probe_infix}"
178	
179	  local z_tag_count=""
180	  z_tag_count=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_probe_infix}" '(.tags // []) | length') \
181	    || buc_die "Failed to count tags for ${z_pkg}"
182	  test "${z_tag_count}" -gt 0 \
183	    || buc_die "No Lode found at ${z_pkg} — nothing to banish"
184	
185	  buc_require "Will banish the whole Lode ${z_pkg} (single packages delete)" "yes"
186	
187	  # Single packages delete removes the package and all its member versions/tags
188	  # atomically. DELETE returns a long-running operation; trust 200 as accepted.
189	  buc_step "Deleting Lode package: ${z_pkg}"
190	  local -r z_del_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}"
191	  local -r z_del_infix="rbld_banish_del"
192	  rbuh_json "DELETE" "${z_del_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_del_infix}"
193	  rbuh_require_ok "Delete Lode package ${z_pkg}" "${z_del_infix}"
194	
195	  echo ""
196	  buc_success "Lode banished: ${z_touchmark}"
197	}
198	
199	# eof
200

content.text: The constant consumers confirm claim 1. The colophon mnemonics (fetter/conclave/underpin/immure) confirm claim 3 — they don't exist anywhere in code. augur exists in spec/RBSLA but let me verify it's absent from the named registries. Let me read the zipper, theurge consts, manifest, the bole body, and the spec sections.

content.content:
80	  buz_enroll RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL        "rbw-dE"  "${z_mod}" "rbfd_enshrine"      "param1"  "Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via Cloud Build"
81	  z_mod="rbfl0_cli.sh"
82	  buz_enroll RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY     "rbw-dI"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_inscribe"      ""  "Create reliquary: mirror tool images from upstream to GAR"
83	  buz_enroll RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY         "rbw-dY"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_yoke"          "param1"  "Yoke a reliquary stamp into every vessel's rbrv.env (wildcard fan-out)"
84	
85	  # Lode — fetched-side universal capture (rbw-l, UPPER=mutates GAR/cost, lower=read-only)

2150	
2151	[[rbtga_touchmark]]
2152	//axl_voices axtg_timestamp
2153	{rbtga_touchmark}::
2154	The identifier marking a captured
2155	{rbtga_lode}
2156	— the fetched-side parallel of the
2157	{rbtga_hallmark}.
2158	Minted host-side before submission, so the touchmark is known before the build (the host owns the stamp in order to write the {rbf_fact_lode}).
2159	+
2160	Format: `«KIND»«YYMMDDHHMMSS»`, where `«KIND»` is the one- or two-letter kind code and the datestamp matches the hallmark's second-granular form.
2161	Kind letters: `b` bole, `t` tool, `r` reliquary, `w` wsl, `vw` podvm-wsl, `vn` podvm-native.
2162	Example: `b260602120000`.
2163	+
2164	The shared `-mark` suffix with {rbtga_hallmark} is a deliberate role-signal: any `*mark` is a specific-instance handle; the first syllable carries the side — `hall-` what we forged, `touch-` what we captured.
2165	The touchmark is the GAR path segment: the Lode package is `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»`, so one value names the whole package.
2166	See {rbst_touchmark} for the string type and regex.
2167	
2168	[[rbtga_lode_vouch]]
2169	//axl_voices axig_verification_artifact
2170	{rbtga_lode_vouch}::
2171	The provenance envelope carried by every
2172	{rbtga_lode}
2173	— one per Lode, batch-level — recording *capture-fidelity* rather than build-integrity.
2174	The fetched-side parallel to the made-side
2175	{rbtga_ark_vouch},
2176	and a different claim: "we faithfully captured upstream X at digest D," not "we built this securely."
2177	+
2178	It has two identical-content homes: the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag (the Lode self-describing in the registry, decoded by {rbtgo_lode_augur}) and the host-side {rbf_fact_lode} (the handoff the consumption-side election reads).
2179	+
2180	Fields (schema `rbld-vouch-1`, near-term unsigned — `signature: null` — but schema-versioned):
2181	`schema`, `kind`, `lode` (the touchmark), `acquired_at` (ISO-8601 UTC), `acquired_by` (the capturing Cloud Build service account), `capture_build` (the capture's own build id), `trust_grade`, and `members[]`.
2182	Each `members[]` entry carries `name`, `origin`, `digest`, `verification`, and its assigned `tags` — so the captured identity and any Director-assigned names are themselves attested.
2183	`members[]` is the cardinality axis: length 1 for singletons (bole), N for cohorts.
2184	+

content.text:
All claims verified against the actual files. Let me record my verdicts.

Every load-bearing claim holds. The recommendation is sound, but I want to flag two nuances on the citations and one substantive point the inventory underweights — the colophon/zipper/theurge-const registration question (claims 3, 4, 5). The investigator correctly found these are absent, but their recommendation calls them "vertical-local, not scaffold," which is a defensible judgment, not a pure fact. I'll note that in corrections without overturning the recommendation.

verdicts.claim: Only RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE="b" is defined; reserved set b/t/r/w/vw/vn is comment-only; constant has exactly 2 consumers besides defn (stamp mint + divine legend).
verdicts.note: Verified rbgc_Constants.sh:217-219 — comment lists 'b bole | t tool | r reliquary | w wsl | vw podvm-wsl | vn podvm-native', only `readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE="b"` exists. grep RBGC_LODE_KIND across Tools/rbk and tt returns exactly defn + rbldb_Bole.sh:197 (stamp) + rbldl_Lifecycle.sh:60 (legend). Exact.

verdicts.claim: GAR layout is a single flat root for all kinds; no per-kind GAR-layout entry; kind lives only in touchmark string.
verdicts.note: rbgl_GarLayout.sh:52 is the only Lode root (`RBGL_LODES_ROOT=RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_LODES`), shape :30 `${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/<kind-letter><stamp>:<member-tag>`. RBS0:2116 confirms single namespace, members ride as tags 'never as path-segment siblings'; :2165 'touchmark is the GAR path segment'; :2118 prior enshrines/reliquaries namespaces 'fold into it as the bole and reliquary kinds land'. No per-kind layout work.

verdicts.claim: Three (actually four) capture colophons missing from zipper: rbw-lF/lC/lU/lI; only ensconce(rbw-lE)/divine(rbw-ld)/banish(rbw-lB) enrolled.
verdicts.note: rbz_zipper.sh:85-90 — RBZ__GROUP_LODE + exactly 3 buz_enroll lines (ENSCONCE_BOLE rbw-lE, DIVINE_LODES rbw-ld, BANISH_LODE rbw-lB). grep fetter/conclave/underpin/immure across Tools/rbk and tt returns nothing in code (only acronym map at claude-rbk-acronyms.md:49). Note: the claim text says 'Three' then lists four mnemonics — minor count slip in the claim's prose, but all four are correctly absent.

verdicts.claim: Three theurge colophon-consts missing in rbtdgc_consts.rs; only ENSCONCE_BOLE/DIVINE_LODES/BANISH_LODE exist (rbw-lE/ld/lB).
verdicts.note: rbtdgc_consts.rs:34-36 — exactly those three consts. No fetter/conclave/underpin/immure/augur const present (grep clean).

verdicts.claim: One shared kind-agnostic lode-lifecycle fixture; no per-kind fixtures; manifest entry is test-coverage choice not slating prerequisite.
verdicts.note: rbtdrm_manifest.rs:56 single RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_LIFECYCLE; :161-165 required colophons = ENSCONCE_BOLE/DIVINE_LODES/BANISH_LODE. Comment :54-55 describes it as kind-agnostic ensconce->divine->banish cycle. Judgment (coverage vs prerequisite) is sound.

verdicts.claim: Divine-legend is the one genuinely kind-coupled registration point besides the kind-letter constant; single hardcoded bole row; each kind adds a row.
verdicts.note: rbldl_Lifecycle.sh:54-60 — comment 'One entry per implemented kind', single `printf ... RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE ... "bole" ...`. This is the one read-side per-kind growth point inside an existing file. Confirmed.

verdicts.claim: divine/banish (and spec'd augur) are kind-agnostic; kind decoded from touchmark leading letter; remaining verticals don't need own divine/banish.
verdicts.note: RBS0:1464 (divine 'Read-only and kind-agnostic — kind decoded from touchmark leading letter'), :1474 (augur 'Read-only and kind-agnostic'). RBSLA-lode_augur.adoc:22-25 'Augur is kind-agnostic ... a new Lode kind needs no change here.' banish (rbldl_Lifecycle.sh:153-197) operates purely on touchmark, no kind branching. Confirmed.

verdicts.claim: Each kind body file (rblds_ spine consumer) is the per-vertical work, not scaffold; bole body mints stamp from RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE:197 and rides spine; RBLDT/R/W/V legend-only, no files.
verdicts.note: rbldb_Bole.sh:197 `z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}..."`, :93-96 calls zrbld_spine_dispatch. ls Tools/rbk confirms only rbld0_cli/rbld0_Lode/rbldb_Bole/rbldl_Lifecycle/rblds_Spine — no rbldt_/rbldr_/rbldw_/rbldv_ files. claude-rbk-acronyms.md:49 'Reserved (legend only, no files this heat): RBLDT tool, RBLDR reliquary/conclave, RBLDW wsl, RBLDV podvm'. Confirmed.

verdicts.claim: augur (rbw-la, rbld_augur) spec'd (RBSLA exists, quoin rbtgo_lode_augur in RBS0) but absent from all code/registries; today single-Lode inspect lives inside rbld_divine via touchmark folio.
verdicts.note: RBS0:151 quoin, :1468-1476 section + include RBSLA-lode_augur.adoc (file exists). grep rbld_augur/augur/rbw-la across rbz_zipper.sh, rbld0_cli.sh, rbldl_Lifecycle.sh, rbtdgc_consts.rs — zero hits. rbldl_Lifecycle.sh:118-151 is the inspect-one-Lode branch inside rbld_divine (folio = touchmark). Fully confirmed.

verdicts.claim: podvm is two kind-letters (vw/vn) — only two-letter codes — so podvm vertical implies TWO kind-letter constants and TWO legend rows; not 1:1 with the 4 kinds.
verdicts.note: rbgc_Constants.sh:218 'vw podvm-wsl | vn podvm-native'; RBS0:2160-2161 'one- or two-letter kind code ... vw podvm-wsl, vn podvm-native'. These are the only two-letter codes. Asymmetry is real.

input.corrections:
The recommendation stands; every load-bearing fact checks out against the files. Three nuances worth surfacing to the planner, none of which overturn the verdict:

1. CLAIM-3 PROSE SLIP (cosmetic, not load-bearing): the claim sentence says "Three capture colophons are MISSING" then lists FOUR (rbw-lF/lC/lU/lI). All four are genuinely absent — the count word is just wrong. No impact on the inventory.

2. THE COLOPHON/ZIPPER/THEURGE-CONST QUESTION IS A JUDGMENT, NOT A FACT (claims 3/4/5 + recommendation item 3). The investigator is factually correct that rbw-lF/lC/lU/lI, their RBZ_ zipper consts, and their RBTDGC_ theurge consts are all absent. But the recommendation's move to file them as "vertical-local, not scaffold" rests on the premise that a capture colophon points at a verb that doesn't yet exist, so it must be authored WITH the body. That premise is sound and I agree with it — flagging only that it is an editorial call about pace boundaries, not a property of the code. A planner who wants the four capture colophons reserved up front (mirroring how the kind-letter constants are promoted up front) could legitimately fold them into the scaffold pace. The recommendation should not present "vertical-local" as forced by the evidence; it's the better of two defensible cuts.

3. THE SCAFFOLD PACE IS EVEN THINNER THAN STATED, and this strengthens the recommendation. The investigator's own analysis concludes the divine-legend rows should grow per-vertical (a legend row for an unimplemented kind would lie — correct, confirmed by rbldl_Lifecycle.sh:56 'One entry per implemented kind'). So of the "THREE coupled edits" listed as genuine residue, item 2 (legend rows) is explicitly deferred per-vertical and item 3 (colophons) is deferred per-vertical. That leaves the scaffold pace as essentially item 1 ALONE: promoting the kind-letter comment block in rbgc_Constants.sh into the 5 readonly constants (t/r/w/vw/vn). The recommendation already reaches this conclusion in its JUDGMENT paragraph, but the inventory's "THREE coupled edits" header overstates what the pace actually contains — the true scaffold pace is a single-file, ~5-line constant promotion. The ONE-serial-pace, lands-first, no-split judgment is correct and well-supported.

4. AUGUR FRAMING IS RIGHT: confirmed via RBSLA:25 ('a new Lode kind needs no change here') that augur is kind-agnostic read infrastructure, not kind-registration residue, and so correctly excluded from the blocking pace and correctly flagged as a distinct optional sibling.

content.content: Structured output provided successfully

content.text: I've verified all ten claims against the actual files. Every load-bearing claim holds. The recommendation stands, with the corrections noting three nuances (one cosmetic count slip, one judgment-vs-fact distinction on the colophon registration question, and one observation that the scaffold pace is actually even thinner than the inventory header suggests) plus confirmation that the augur framing is correct.
